seoTitle: How to Export Data From Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Export your Slack workspace history as a ZIP archive through the admin dashboard for backups, compliance, or migration needs.
intro: Securing compliance logs or backing up your workspace before a major cleanup requires the right admin tools. This guide covers accessing the export dashboard, selecting a date range, and downloading your archived data.

steps.heading: Open Workspace Settings
steps.body: Click your workspace name, select Settings & Administration, then choose Workspace Settings.

steps.heading: Find the Import/Export section
steps.body: Scroll down the left-hand navigation ribbon and click 'Import/Export Data'.

steps.heading: Select the Export tab
steps.body: Choose the Export panel to review your current history retention parameters.

steps.heading: Choose a date range
steps.body: Click the date range dropdown to select a preset time block or set custom start and end dates.

steps.heading: Start and download the export
steps.body: Click 'Start Export', wait for the email confirmation, then click the download link to save the ZIP archive locally.

faq.question: Who has permission to export workspace data?
faq.answer: Only workspace admins or owners typically have access to the data export dashboard.

faq.question: Does a standard export include private channel messages?
faq.answer: Standard exports usually cover public channel data, while private and direct message content often requires a compliance export plan.

faq.question: How long does an export take to process?
faq.answer: Processing time depends on workspace size, but you'll get an email notification once the archive is ready.

faq.question: What file format is the exported data in?
faq.answer: Exports are typically delivered as a ZIP file containing structured JSON logs of your workspace history.
